related_questions.question: Does iOS manage RAM better than Android?
related_questions.answer: On average, iOS apps actually use far less memory than their Android counterparts — testing shows roughly 40% less RAM needed for the same apps on iPhone. One reason is language overhead: Android apps often run on a Java virtual machine (JVM), which adds memory overhead, whereas iOS apps are compiled to native code.Jan 1, 2026
related_questions.answer_highlight: iOS apps actually use far less memory than their Android counterparts

source.title: Why Apple Devices Feel Smooth Even With Lower RAM? - Medium
